pages.raw_text:
A ruling by the attorney general
holds that state funds appropri-
ated for the school for feeble mind
ed youth at Medical Lake cannot
be used for the care of children of
this class in King county. The in
stitution at Medical Lake is filled,
and the applicants from King
county were cared for there.


Twcnty-eiehth avenue, Perry to
Arthur. and Thirty-third avenue,
from Perry street to 800 feet west,
have been ordered graded ana
walks laid. Plans are ready for
the paving of Howard street, Fifth
to Seventh. A petition for the
grading and sidewalking Of Rich
avenue, Pittsburg to Crestline, has
been placed ON file.


Rev. Mel Trotter, an exponent of
militant Christianity, will be in
Spokane February 20 and will con-
duct two revival meetings at the
Armory. Mel is the founder of the
Rescue mission.


Tim Harrlgan, who stepped into
a hole on Pacific avenue, has
asked the city to pay him damages
to the extent of $500. No lights
were burning. he claims, and as
the result of the accident he sus-
tanned sprained ankle that has In
capacitated him from labor.


Howard street property owners,
Fifth to Seventh, have protested
against the narrowing of that
street to 80 feet, claiming that a
wider street is necessary because
of the traffic.


A rate of $12.5o for the round
trip to Seattle from Rosalia has
been made by the Milwaukee for
the opening of the Western Steel
company, January 81. A number
of Inland Empire people will go.


While In Spokane yesterday
State Bank Examiner j. l.. Mohun-
dro stated that the next legislature
will be asked to pass law putting
out of business all private banks
which refuse to take out a charter
under the state law. There are 1856
state banks and "A national banks
in Washington, and over 60 per
cent of the bank deposits of the
state are held in the state banks.
The bank examiner finds that the
banks of the state are In a healthy
condition.


Governor M. E. Hay and W. D..
Stem, Bible class organizer of Kan
sas City. were honor guests at a
banquet given last night in the in
terests of the Adult Bible class
campaign in the lower dining room
of the Chamber of Commerce.
About $50 men and women attended
the affair and enjoyed the adj
dresses made by Governor Hay and
Mr.. Stem.


At a meeting held last evening at
Opportunity, County Superintend-
ent Edward /. Mcfarland spoke ON
the consolidation of the schools all
over the country. At the meeting
last night school patrons were
present from Orchard avenue,
Trent, Chester, Vera and East Spo.
kane.
